title: Spicy Thai Carrot Crumble
description: A rich, gooey meal-prep dish featuring self-raising flour that's both diabetic-friendly and gluten-free. The addition of curry paste adds a spicy kick to this comforting breakfast.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine self-raising flour, grated carrots, coconut milk, almond milk, curry paste, and salt. Stir until well combined.
Fold in ground cumin gently to avoid over-mixing which can cause the mixture to fall apart.
Heat about 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a non-stick skillet or frying pan over medium heat.
Once heated, pour in the carrot crumble mixture and use spatula to spread evenly across the pan. Cook for about 5-7 minutes until golden brown on bottom.
Gently flip the crumbles with spatula and cook for another 2-3 minutes until both sides are golden and slightly crispy.
Remove from heat, transfer to a plate lined with paper towel to absorb extra oil, then serve warm as your breakfast.
